svg.title: PLC 1 Breadboard Layout - Arduino Mega + All Modules
svg.text: PLC 1 Breadboard Layout: Arduino Mega + All Modules
Use a full-size breadboard (or two half-size). Wire power rails first.
Direct wire to Arduino (no breadboard needed)

g.text: a) Wire Arduino 5V → breadboard top + rail
b) Wire Arduino GND → breadboard top - rail
c) Wire Arduino 3.3V → breadboard bottom orange rail (for RFID only)
d) Jumper: top GND rail → bottom GND rail (so both halves share ground)
a) Wire Arduino D50 (MISO) → breadboard column 1, any row in top half
b) Wire Arduino D51 (MOSI) → breadboard column 2, any row in top half
c) Wire Arduino D52 (SCK) → breadboard column 3, any row in top half
These 3 columns now carry SPI signals. Both W5500 and RFID tap into them.
a) Push W5500 header pins into columns 5-8
b) Jumper: W5500 MISO pin row → column 1 same row (connects to SPI bus)
c) Jumper: W5500 MOSI pin row → column 2 same row
d) Jumper: W5500 SCK pin row → column 3 same row
e) Wire: W5500 CS pin row → Arduino D10
f) Jumper: W5500 VCC row → + rail, GND row → - rail
g) Plug Ethernet cable from W5500 RJ45 → Switch Port 2
a) Push MAX485 into columns 10-13. Wire VCC row → + rail, GND row → - rail
b) Wire: RO → Arduino D19 (RX1), DI → Arduino D18 (TX1), DE+RE → Arduino D2
c) Connect A and B terminals → twisted pair cable → PLC 2 MAX485
a) Push RFID into bottom half of breadboard. Jumper SPI pins to columns 1-3 (bottom half)
b) RFID VCC → 3.3V rail (orange). NOT 5V! The MFRC522 will be damaged by 5V.
c) Wire: SDA → Arduino D53 (CS), RST → Arduino D5, GND → GND rail
STEP 6: Actuators + LCD (direct wiring, no breadboard)
Relay(IN→D8), Servo(Signal→D9), Buzzer(+→D12). LCD: RS→D22, E→D23, D4-D7→D24-D27. Power from breadboard rails.
